2603.15988.title: Something from Nothing: Data Augmentation for Robust Severity Level Estimation of Dysarthric Speech
2603.15988.paper_summary: Dysarthric speech quality assessment (DSQA) is critical for clinical diagnostics and inclusive speech technologies. However, subjective evaluation is costly and difficult to scale, and the scarcity of labeled data limits robust objective modeling. To address this, we propose a three-stage framework that leverages unlabeled dysarthric speech and large-scale typical speech datasets to scale training. A teacher model first generates pseudo-labels for unlabeled samples, followed by weakly supervised pretraining using a label-aware contrastive learning strategy that exposes the model to diverse speakers and acoustic conditions. The pretrained model is then fine-tuned for the downstream DSQA task. Experiments on five unseen datasets spanning multiple etiologies and languages demonstrate the robustness of our approach. Our Whisper-based baseline significantly outperforms SOTA DSQA predictors such as SpICE, and the full framework achieves an average SRCC of 0.761 across unseen test datasets.
2603.15988.paper_summary_zh: 語音障礙評估 (DSQA) 對於臨床診斷和包容性語音技術至關重要。<lb/>然而，主觀評估成本高且難以擴展，而標記數據的稀缺限制了穩健的客觀建模。<lb/>為了解決這個問題，我們提出了一個三階段框架，利用未標記的語音障礙樣本和大規模的典型語音數據集來擴展訓練。<lb/>教師模型首先為未標記樣本生成偽標籤，接著使用標籤感知對比學習策略進行弱監督的預訓練，這使模型接觸到多樣的說話者和聲學條件。<lb/>然後，預訓練模型將針對下游的 DSQA 任務進行微調。<lb/>在五個涵蓋多種病因和語言的未見數據集上的實驗顯示了我們方法的穩健性。<lb/>我們基於 Whisper 的基線顯著超越了 SOTA DSQA 預測器，如 SpICE，並且完整框架在未見測試數據集上達到了平均 SRCC 0.761。
2603.15988.author: Jaesung Bae et.al.
2603.15988.authors: Jaesung Bae, Xiuwen Zheng, Minje Kim, Chang D. Yoo, Mark Hasegawa-Johnson
